message:
fix(io): always clean up local waker state on deregister and retry in Drop

deregister(): Restructure to always remove the waker from the slab
and clear the interest map regardless of whether the reactor
deregister call succeeds. Previously, a reactor error (e.g., EBADF
from a prematurely closed fd) would short-circuit before cleaning
up local state, leaking the waker entry in the slab. The generation
counter in SlabToken already prevents ABA issues, so cleaning up
eagerly is safe. The reactor result is still returned to the caller
for error reporting after local cleanup completes.

try_turn_with(): Remove unnecessary mut qualifier on the on_event
callback parameter, since it is passed by move to
restore_and_extract_wakers which takes FnMut.

IoRegistration::Drop: Add one retry for transient (non-NotFound)
deregister failures. The previous Drop impl made a single
best-effort deregister call. If the reactor returned a transient
error (e.g., EINTR, EAGAIN), the registration would be abandoned
with a stale entry in both the reactor and the driver. Now the
Drop path checks the first result and retries once if the error
is transient, reducing the window for leaked registrations.

New test: io_registration_drop_retries_transient_deregister_error
verifies that Drop issues exactly 2 deregister calls when the
FlakyReactor fails once then succeeds.

Updated test: io_registration_deregister_persistent_error_returns_err
now expects the driver to be empty after persistent failure (since
deregister always cleans up local state) and expects 2 total calls
(not 3, since Drop no longer makes an additional attempt when
deregister() already exhausted retries).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
